channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Governo propõe aos estados subsídio na importação de diesel em R$ 1,20 por litro
item.description: The federal government proposed to states a subsidy of R$1.20 per liter on diesel imports, split equally between the Union and states (R$0.60 each), as an alternative to cutting the ICMS tax. The measure aims to mitigate the impact of rising oil prices on fuel costs and is expected to generate a R$3 billion effect within two months, with a decision anticipated at the Confaz meeting on Friday the 27th.

item.title: PL dos apps: governo propõe mínimo de R$ 10 por corrida e pagamento integral por rota agrupada
item.description: The federal government proposed a bill establishing a minimum payment of R$10 per ride for app-based workers, with an additional R$2.50 per km for trips over 4 km, plus full payment for grouped deliveries. The Working Group, composed of delivery and ride-hailing worker representatives and seven ministries, presented the report as an alternative to the bill currently under discussion in Congress. Minister Guilherme Boulos also announced two ordinances: one to install 100 support points with basic infrastructure for workers, and another to ensure transparency in delivery pricing, detailing earnings for workers, platforms, and restaurants.

item.title: Moraes concede domiciliar temporária, mas suspende visitas a Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted former President Jair Bolsonaro a temporary 90-day house arrest for humanitarian reasons but suspended all scheduled visits during this period to prevent infection risks amid his recovery from bilateral bacterial pneumonia. The ruling mandates an electronic ankle monitor, allows visits from his sons under set conditions, and permits medical care without prior judicial approval.

item.title: Domiciliar de Bolsonaro: Moraes dá a Michelle "vitória" sobre Flávio
item.description: Alexandre de Moraes authorized house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro one day after meeting with Michelle Bolsonaro, with her allies interpreting the timing as a symbolic 'victory' over Flávio Bolsonaro, who had visited the minister a week earlier. Internal family tensions persist over who will be the Bolsonaro clan’s presidential candidate in 2026, as Michelle seeks to be the ticket’s head or vice-presidential nominee.

item.title: Comissão aprova moção de aplausos para ex-PF que prendeu Lula em 2018
item.description: The Public Security Committee of the Chamber of Deputies approved a motion of applause for former federal police officer Danilo Campetti, a São Paulo state deputy, for his role in Operation Car Wash, including the coercive detention and arrest of Lula in 2018. The proposal, introduced by Bolsonarist deputy Paulo Bilynskyj, praised his 'discipline, courage, and commitment to legality.' Campetti had previously been investigated and temporarily removed from the PF over alleged improper political activity during Tarcísio de Freitas’ 2022 campaign.

item.title: Fazenda propõe medida para reduzir preço do diesel em R$ 1,20
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan proposed a R$ 1.20 per liter subsidy on imported diesel, costing R$ 3 billion split equally between the federal government and states, effective for March and April 2026. The measure aims to lower diesel prices, which reached R$ 7.26/liter, addressing complaints from mayors and governors about fuel shortages following the Middle East war, and replaces a previously studied ICMS tax reduction.

item.title: Por reajuste, policiais penais protestam contra Tarcísio na Alesp
item.description: Penitentiary police officers in São Paulo protest at the State Legislative Assembly against Governor Tarcísio de Freitas’ administration, demanding the same 10% salary increase granted to the Civil, Military and Scientific Police. The union claims they were excluded from the governor’s legislative proposals and lost career benefits such as quinquenniums and sexta-parte in a prior restructuring.

item.title: PL Antifacção: juristas pedem veto a audiências de custódia virtuais
item.description: Over 300 jurists from the Institute for the Defense of the Right of Defense (IDDD) urged President Lula to veto the provision in the Anti-Faction Bill making virtual custody hearings mandatory, arguing it facilitates torture and violates fundamental rights. The group cites a 2025 study showing that physical judicial presence increases detainee protection and notes that in-person hearings saved R$400 million in 2015 by reducing prison system costs, according to the National Justice Council. The bill will be signed by Lula on Tuesday (March 24), with expectations of few vetoes.

item.title: Gostamos de dar a chave à mulher, diz Lula sobre Minha Casa, Minha Vida
item.description: President Lula stated during a ceremony delivering 2,215 homes under the Minha Casa, Minha Vida program that he prefers handing keys to women, arguing it reduces domestic violence by 'locking the husband outside' if he gets angry. He also emphasized that homeownership ensures educational stability for children, avoiding frequent school changes due to renting. The event was held remotely from the Planalto, with officials present in Santarém, Dias d’Ávila, Rio Largo, and São Brás.

item.title: Gabriel Sara cita Diniz e mira chance dada por Ancelotti na seleção: ‘Surpresa para mim’
item.description: Gabriel Sara, called up for the first time to the Brazilian national team, credited Fernando Diniz as a father figure in his career and expressed surprise at the opportunity given by Carlo Ancelotti. The Galatasaray midfielder described the call-up as a life opportunity and aims to impress in the friendlies against France and Croatia to secure a spot in the 2026 World Cup roster.

item.title: Impasse com partidos de centro aproxima Rodrigo Pacheco do PSB
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) is negotiating to join the PSB and run for governor of Minas Gerais in 2026, after stalled talks with MDB and União Brasil over national positioning. His candidacy requires a party neutral in the presidential election and with state-level autonomy, aiming to secure a Lula-aligned ticket in Minas. Vice Governor Mateus Simões (PSD), appointed by Romeu Zema (Novo), blocks Pacheco from staying in the PSD to run in the state.

item.title: Moraes concede prisão domiciliar a Bolsonaro para se proteger
item.description: Minister Alexandre de Moraes granted house arrest for 90 days to Jair Bolsonaro, convicted of leading a coup attempt, citing delicate health conditions. The decision, as described, reflects political calculations and institutional self-preservation by the Supreme Federal Court rather than legal or humanitarian grounds, amid Moraes’ involvement in the Banco Master scandal and fear of backlash if Bolsonaro deteriorates under his custody.

item.title: O motivo por trás de ausência de Michelle em filiação de Moro ao PL
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro's absence from the PL affiliation event of Sergio Moro and Rosângela Moro was explained by PL president Valdemar Costa Neto due to Jair Bolsonaro's hospital discharge and transfer to temporary house arrest. Michelle had confirmed her attendance to honor Rosângela Moro, president of PL Women, but could not attend due to the ex-president's special treatment requirements. She had met with STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes the previous day to advocate for house arrest on health grounds, which was granted on Tuesday.

item.title: A primeira derrota do governo Lula no STF
item.description: The Lula administration suffered its first defeat in the Supreme Federal Court (STF) after repeatedly relying on the Court to overturn congressional losses. The STF's ruling ended expectations that the INSS CPMI would be shut down, a move the government had treated as certain. This marks a turning point in the administration’s strategy of using the Judiciary as a political refuge against congressional opposition.

item.title: Caiado anuncia renúncia ao governo de Goiás para viabilizar candidatura à Presidência
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) announced he will resign from office on the 31st to pursue a potential presidential candidacy in 2026. He traveled to São Paulo to meet with PSD President Gilberto Kassab to discuss the next steps of his campaign.

item.title: O primeiro ato de desembargador à frente do governo do Rio
item.description: Acting Rio de Janeiro Governor Ricardo Couto, also president of the state’s Court of Justice, dismissed Nicola Miccione from the Civil House and appointed Marco Antônio Rodrigues Simões as the new secretary, complying with STF Minister Luiz Fux’s six-month disqualification rule. Miccione, favored by Governor Cláudio Castro to run for a placeholder mandate in the state legislature, was ineligible due to insufficient time away from office. Couto met with STF Minister Edson Fachin in Brasília on his first day in office.

item.title: Moraes fixa prazo de 90 dias para domiciliar a Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes authorized house arrest for former President Jair Bolsonaro on humanitarian grounds, granting the defense's request. The measure follows the completion of his sentence for attempted coup in November 2025. A 90-day deadline was set for the implementation of the house arrest.

item.title: Moraes manda Bolsonaro para prisão domiciliar por 90 dias
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted Jair Bolsonaro's defense request for house arrest in Brasília for 90 days, with a subsequent review. The decision was issued today, accepting the defense's petition.

item.title: Motta defende voto distrital misto como forma de aprimorar sistema eleitoral
item.description: Hugo Motta, president of the Chamber of Deputies, advocated for mixed-district voting as a way to make the electoral system more participatory, democratic, and low-cost, stating it meets public demand for more committed representatives. He said he plans to restart political reform talks with party leaders after the party window closes in early April, and highlighted legislative priorities such as regulating app-based workers, AI, and critical minerals. Motta also stressed the importance of combating misinformation through improved legislation — without censorship — to ensure clean elections and freedom of expression with clear rules.

item.title: Lula convoca última reunião ministerial para 30 de março
item.description: President Lula has called the final ministerial meeting for March 30, 2026, to align political strategy ahead of the October elections, in which 21 ministers are expected to leave office to run for elected positions. The meeting, to be held at the Palácio do Planalto, will have a farewell tone and serve to define replacements, prioritizing technical executive secretaries, as 14 ministries still lack confirmed substitutes and 16 ministers await meetings with Lula to determine the future of their portfolios.

item.title: Ratinho Junior perdeu o prazo para ser vice de Flávio, diz Sóstenes
item.description: The leader of the PL in the Chamber, Sóstenes Cavalcante, stated that the deadline for Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior to become Flávio Bolsonaro’s running mate had expired last week. Ratinho Junior decided not to run for president in 2026, choosing to remain in office until December due to the risk of failing to secure a successor in Paraná and Moro’s growing influence in the state. Although considered a favorite within the PSD, he will not contest the party’s presidential nomination, adhering to an internal agreement to support the chosen candidate.

item.title: Lula participa de entrega simultânea de moradias do Minha Casa, Minha Vida
item.description: President Lula (PT) participated in the simultaneous delivery of 2,215 housing units under the Minha Casa, Minha Vida program across four states (Pará, Bahia, Alagoas, and the Federal District), benefiting over 8,800 people. The ceremony took place at the Palácio do Planalto, with coordinated events in Santarém (PA), Dias d’Ávila (BA), Rio Largo (AL), and São Brás (AL), involving Minister of Cities Jader Filho and funding from the Residential Leasing Fund and the Union’s General Budget.

item.title: Castro pode ficar fora das eleições em caso de condenação no TSE apesar de renúncia do cargo
item.description: Cláudio Castro resigned as governor of Rio de Janeiro on Monday, days before a TSE ruling that could make him ineligible until 2030. While his resignation prevents removal from office, the court may still apply a penalty of ineligibility if it finds the charges proven. His departure triggers an indirect election to choose a new governor, with the president of the Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice serving as interim due to a dual vacancy in the executive.

item.title: "Não entendi o movimento", diz Flávio sobre Ratinho Jr. no Planalto
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) said he did not understand Governor Ratinho Júnior's (PSD-PR) presidential pre-candidacy, stating there was no reason for the move. He praised the governor as a 'great leader' and 'good figure' for national alliances, but said the withdrawal was surprising. Flávio also commented on Sergio Moro's PL affiliation and Moro's selection as the party's pre-candidate for governor of Paraná.

item.title: Não é a guerra que amedronta os economistas do Brasil, mas um outro perigo real
item.description: The Copom minutes were uneventful, but economists’ real concern is the potential testimony of Daniel Vorcaro, seen as a direct threat to the Brazilian real and assets, capable of destabilizing expectations amid tense elections. André Perfeito advocates aggressive rate cuts, arguing current credit conditions are silently eroding businesses and households, while Ricardo Rocha agrees with the diagnosis but opposes off-cycle moves, citing the BC’s inherent caution amid political volatility. The Middle East conflict has receded in relevance as domestic political risk now poses a more immediate and direct threat to exchange rates, inflation, and interest rates.

item.title: Com interino no comando, RJ fará eleição para mandato tampão; entenda
item.description: The resignation of Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) led to Judge Ricardo Couto assuming the interim governorship of Rio de Janeiro, following the vacancy of the vice-governor and the absence of the Alerj president. An indirect election (buffer mandate) will be held within 30 days to select a governor to serve until the October 2026 inauguration. The TSE resumed proceedings that could result in Castro’s removal and eight-year ineligibility for alleged abuse of economic and political power in the 2022 re-election campaign.

item.title: Ratinho Jr, desiste de ser candidato e Caiado deve sair a presidente pelo PSD
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. withdrew his candidacy for president under the PSD, opening space for Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado to become the party's presidential nominee. The decision followed a meeting of PSD leaders in São Paulo, including national party president Gilberto Kassab. The news was reported by UOL on March 24, 2026, emphasizing its impact on the opposition's electoral strategy.

item.title: Caiado quer colocar o bloco na rua já
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado traveled urgently to São Paulo to meet with Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, to discuss his potential 2026 presidential candidacy. The meeting will cover party structure, campaign planning, and financing for Caiado’s political project. The encounter was confirmed by O Antagonista.

item.title: Moro insinua que vitória de Lula sobre Bolsonaro foi ilegítima
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro insinuated without providing evidence that Lula's victory over Bolsonaro in the 2022 election was illegitimate during his affiliation with the PL on March 24, 2026. He claimed the current president is on the side of criminals and minimizes crime, repeating Bolsonarist narratives despite his Lava Jato convictions being annulled by the STF in 2021. The statement was made alongside Flávio Bolsonaro and Valdemar Costa Neto, marking his formal alignment with bolsonarismo as a pré-candidate for governor of Paraná.

item.title: STF rejeita novos recursos do núcleo de desinformação da trama golpista
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) unanimously rejected appeals from the seven defendants in the disinformation nucleus of the coup plot, upholding their convictions for involvement in the post-2022 election attempt to overturn democracy, including spreading fake news and misusing the Abin to monitor authorities. The defendants were found to have sought to create political instability to justify military intervention and keep Jair Bolsonaro in power.

item.title: Edinho sobre Flávio moderado: "É legítimo quererem construir um tipo"
item.description: The national president of the Workers' Party (PT), Edinho Silva, described Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) moderate profile in the pre-campaign as a 'marketing strategy', stating it is legitimate for candidates to want to 'build an image' during elections, but emphasized voters must examine the candidate's history. He argued that Flávio represents the Bolsonaro family legacy and warned against forgetting Brazil under Bolsonaro's governance, despite current rebranding efforts.

item.title: O que novas pesquisas revelam sobre Lula x Flávio na região que define os rumos da eleição
item.description: Recent Real Time Big Data polls show a tight race between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in the Southeast, which holds 42% of the electorate and is pivotal for the 2026 election. In Minas Gerais and São Paulo, the candidates are technically tied, while Flávio leads narrowly in Rio de Janeiro and Espírito Santo. The contest is increasingly driven by voter rejection rather than policy proposals, with Lula burdened by corruption scandals and Flávio facing challenges in solidifying his base.

item.title: Bolsonaro está internado recebendo antibioticoterapia, sem previsão de deixar hospital, diz boletim
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro remains hospitalized at DF Star since March 13 due to bilateral bacterial pneumonia, receiving intravenous antibiotic therapy and clinical support. There is no forecast for discharge, and he is serving a 27-year-and-three-month sentence for attempting a coup to stay in power after the 2022 elections. Allies and the Prosecutor General’s Office are pushing for house arrest, with Minister Alexandre de Moraes of the STF set to decide, following a favorable opinion from the PGR.

item.title: Michelle faz publicação enigmática após encontro com Moraes
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro posted an enigmatic message on Instagram after visiting Minister Alexandre de Moraes, stating that 'not every provocation deserves a response'. The meeting followed a favorable opinion from the PGR regarding house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro, who is hospitalized with bronchopneumonia. Allies interpreted the post as an indirect comment on differences in her approach compared to Flávio Bolsonaro in defending the former president.

item.title: Em filiaçăo ao PL, Moro diz a Flávio Bolsonaro: 'Paraná năo vai lhe faltar'
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro, upon joining the Liberal Party (PL) in Brasília, stated he will provide his political platform in Paraná to support Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) 2026 presidential campaign. The remark was made during his affiliation ceremony, indicating political alignment between the two.

item.title: Quem é Ricardo Couto, que assume governo do Rio após renúncia de Castro
item.description: Judge Ricardo Couto assumed the governorship of Rio de Janeiro following the resignation of Cláudio Castro, who faced potential removal by the TSE over alleged abuse of power in the 2022 elections. Couto, president of the Rio State Court of Justice, will serve only until an indirect election in the Legislative Assembly determines an interim governor, without planning administrative changes or seeking to extend his term, amid judicial uncertainty over succession rules and the STF's pending decision.

item.title: ‘Verão do Flávio’ pulveriza favoritismo de Lula
item.description: The 'Summer of Flávio' marks the strengthening of Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-candidacy, with rising poll numbers and consolidated bolsonarist support, while Lula faces declining popularity, scandals like the Master case, and internal PT challenges. A potential house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro could further unify the bolsonarist base behind Flávio, and the rise of Ronaldo Caiado as a 'third way' alternative adds complexity to the 2026 electoral landscape.

item.title: Deltan Dallagnol é anunciado pré-candidato ao Senado pelo Paraná em evento de filiação de Sergio Moro ao PL
item.description: Deltan Dallagnol was announced as a pre-candidate for the Senate from Paraná during Sergio Moro's affiliation event with the PL, forming a 'Lava Jato' ticket. Despite having his candidacy registration annulled in 2023, Dallagnol remains eligible to run in 2026. The event also confirmed Filipe Barros as a Senate candidate on the same ticket and marked the PL's break with Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior, who withdrew his presidential pre-candidacy.

item.title: Supermercados já podem vender medicamentos; entenda
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva signed Law No. 15.357, authorizing supermarkets to install pharmacies in segregated, exclusive areas, provided they meet sanitary and technical requirements, including the mandatory presence of licensed pharmacists. The law prohibits selling medicines in open or non-segregated areas and restricts controlled medications to post-payment delivery in sealed, tamper-evident packaging. It also permits licensed pharmacies to use digital platforms for logistics and delivery, as long as full sanitary compliance is maintained.

item.title: Como a decisão abrupta de Ratinho Júnior bagunça o plano do PSD para 2026
item.description: Ratinho Júnior's abrupt withdrawal from the presidential race exposed weaknesses in the PSD's internal coordination and underscored the structural challenges to building a viable third way in Brazil amid institutional polarization and declining trust in institutions. His decision, driven by family pressures and the threat posed by Sergio Moro's entry in Paraná, prioritized his state-level influence over a nationally risky campaign. The move left other PSD presidential hopefuls, such as Ronaldo Caiado and Eduardo Leite, in an uncomfortable position and highlighted the lack of a clear path for a competitive alternative candidacy in 2026.

item.title: Entenda o que acontece no Rio com a renúncia de Claudio Castro
item.description: Claudio Castro resigned as governor of Rio de Janeiro to run for the Senate in the October elections, following the 2025 departure of Vice Governor Thiago Pampolha and the leave of absence of Alerj President Rodrigo Bacellar, who was arrested in December 2025 for allegedly leaking confidential information in Operation Unha e Carne. TJ-RJ President Ricardo Couto assumed interim governance, and the TSE will resume the trial that could disqualify Castro for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 re-election campaign, with a current vote of 2-0 in favor of cassation.

item.title: Moro se filia ao PL e mira o governo do Paraná
item.description: Senator Sérgio Moro officially joined the Liberal Party (PL) on March 24, 2025, after leaving União Brasil, and confirmed his intention to run for governor of Paraná in 2026. The move follows weeks of negotiations and internal resistance in his former party. His affiliation with the PL strengthens his alignment with the conservative and Bolsonarist camp.

item.title: Silvia Abravanel se filia ao PSD para concorrer a deputada federal em SP
item.description: Television presenter Silvia Abravanel, daughter of Silvio Santos, is joining the PSD to run for federal deputy from São Paulo. The affiliation ceremony will take place this Tuesday at 3 p.m. at the party’s headquarters in São Paulo. She follows her father’s political path, who ran for president in 1989 under the PMB, a candidacy later invalidated by the TSE due to party registration irregularities and his ownership of a public service concession.

item.title: Davi Kopenawa cobra julgamento no TSE que pode cassar Denarium
item.description: Indigenous leader Davi Kopenawa delivered a letter to TSE President Cármen Lúcia demanding the urgent judgment of a case that could remove Roraima Governor Antonio Denarium for abuse of power in the 2022 elections. He links Denarium’s continued tenure to the expansion of illegal mining in Yanomami territory, which brings disease, death, mercury contamination, and environmental destruction. The case, initiated in 2024 at the TSE, has been stalled by requests for review despite a favorable vote for cassation by relator Isabel Gallotti in August 2025.

item.title: Lula foi eleito “entre aspas” e apoia criminosos, diz Moro
item.description: Senator Sérgio Moro launched his pre-candidacy for governor of Paraná and officially joined the PL party, claiming Lula was elected 'in quotes' and is aligned with criminals, while criticizing the government's economy as chaotic and claiming corruption has returned on an unimaginable scale, even stealing from retirees. Moro also said he expects Jair Bolsonaro to be placed under house arrest for 'a matter of justice' and reiterated the need to defeat Lula, stating his election brought a shadow over the country worse than he had imagined.

item.title: Em filiação de Moro ao PL, Flávio Bolsonaro diz que Lula 'passa mão na cabeça de bandido'
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president, criticized President Lula for allegedly 'patting bandits on the head' during Sergio Moro's affiliation event with the PL. He reaffirmed his intention to harden the public security agenda, arguing that 'marginals' must be treated firmly.

item.title: Em filiação de Moro ao PL, Flávio Bolsonaro diz que Lula ‘passa mão na cabeça de bandido’
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) criticized President Lula, claiming his government 'pampers criminals' and advocated for a tougher public security agenda. He reaffirmed his support for Sergio Moro’s affiliation with the PL and announced alliances with Filipe Barros and Deltan Dallagnol for Senate seats in Paraná, emphasizing the state’s strategic importance for his 2026 presidential bid.

item.title: Arrecadação com casas de apostas sobe 236% em 2026, diz Fisco
item.description: Brazil's revenue from betting houses surged 236% in the first two months of 2026, reaching R$ 2.5 billion, driven by the full implementation of the fixed-odds betting market regulation since 2025. The increase reflects an expanded tax base and improved oversight by the Federal Revenue Service, with the tax on Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR) rising from 12% to 13% in 2026, as approved by Congress through PLP 128/2025.

item.title: Por dentro da suposta operação russa para fomentar protestos contra o governo em Angola
item.description: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, were arrested in Angola in August 2023 and now face 11 charges, including terrorism, espionage, and influence peddling, for alleged involvement in an operation by Africa Politology, a network linked to the defunct Wagner Group, aimed at fomenting protests and interfering in Angola’s 2024 presidential election. The defense denies any ties to the Russian state or Wagner, claiming the men were merely working to establish a 'Russian House' cultural center in Luanda. The case underscores rising tensions between Russia and Angola, a major oil and diamond producer that has been gradually distancing itself from Russian influence since the Cold War.

item.title: Flávio lança Moro e diz ser o verdadeiro defensor da democracia
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro launched Sérgio Moro as the PL's pre-candidate for governor of Paraná and announced Moro's official affiliation with the party. He claimed his political group represents the "true defenders of democracy" and criticized the Lula government, alleging it is delivering more misery, dependence, and corruption. Flávio stressed alignment with Moro on public security, emphasizing crime-fighting as central to the state race.
